title: NME and NGE Gendo Seele Powers That Be SPLIT
display_title: NME and NGE Gendo Seele Powers That Be SPLIT
wikitext:
[]: NME & NGE: Gendo/Seele/Powers That Be SPLIT             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 04, 2012 8:43 am Split from here .~ SSD Na7e wrote: That isn't fact though. Your going off of speculation. What you said there hasn't been confirmed.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Jan 04, 2012 9:45 am This thread is fast becoming a mass of tangents, but i just can't let the Gendo topic go, and i don't feel like opening a new thred somewhere else. Some bullet points: 1., The two main qualities that make Gendo useful to have around at Nerv from Seele's point of view are his knowledge of the H.I. Project and the fact that he is a ruthless mofo'. These factors combined actually make him the biggest threat to H.I.P. should he get second thoughts, which is what he does, and Seele can see that. 2., His contributions to "saving the world" are saying "a" and "kamawan" every now and then. The only stunt he actually pulled to this end was having the LoL deployed. He is not essential for keeping the Evas and the associated equipment working, and he is not essential in a tactical situation. Once again, his main role is to be chaperone for H.I.P., and that's exactly what he is jeopardizing. Removing him wouldn't really hinder Nerv's ability to defeat the Angels, which is a required, though secondary goal for Seele. I mean just looking at EoE, if Rei didn't say "No", he would have won, and Seele couldn't have done anything about it. I just don't see where the advantages of keeping him alive balance the risks involved with doing so. 3., As for his replacement: a, Bring over a more reliable commander from another branch of Nerv. b, Promote Fuyutski. Plant a bomb in his heart, just to be safe. Really, Seele is the lousiest ancient conspiracy ever.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 04, 2012 10:10 am SaltyJoe wrote: This thread is fast becoming a mass of tangents, but i just can't let the Gendo topic go, and i don't feel like opening a new thred somewhere else.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jan 04, 2012 4:14 pm Re: IPEA/EU: IPEA =/= EU. The fact that the IPEA could drag Unit 02 out of storage under Gendo's nose has nothing to do with the EU. It does not support the notion that Ritsuko had the right of things when she said it was the EU's decision because, as noted previously, Gendo made their input moot. Re: Evas Aren't Dangerous: The fourth division of the JSSDF would respectfully disagree. Once you give those suckers batteries (or, worse, S2 engines) the argument becomes hysterical (and in NME, given the development of Units 06 and 08 it's clear the U.N. knew about the possibility of independent Evas at the time. Berserker units just make things that much messier). But even apart from that they're ideal, and reusable, bunker busters. The AT Field will cut through anything, and it makes them indestructible. They really are the most dangerous weapons on the planet. Re: Replacing Gendo: What Jorno and Alaska said. He was too deeply entrenched by that point to be easily replaced, and SEELE didn't have any other candidates available that had the requisite knowledge to do what needed to be done and were also loyal. Also, they said outright that they needed him so this is a moot issue. And again, having elements of your command staff harassed before your entire organization is dismantled as soon as it's no longer useful is not "no consequences." Stop looking for immediate gratification.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Wed Jan 04, 2012 6:15 pm The fourth division also placed a naval vessel in a lake on top of a de-activated Eva. That's not military strategy. That's a set piece so 02 can lift a ship out of the water. If they were smart all it would take was a dive team. Plant a nuke on Unit-02. Detonate. Bye-bye Asuka. They're incredibly fragile like any piece of military equipment deactivated. Also engaging possible Evangelion units in a giant dome. Where you have nowhere to run. That's not exactly how I would try and, take down an Eva. Yes, they're AT-fields make them strong. The power issue has been solved. I still make the case they're awful. You can see them miles away. A berserk unit is even worse. It would attack anything it see's hence why it's called a 'berserker'. Why use a unit that could turn on you. Even then we've only seen two cases of it. Those being a prototype, Test-type, and a provisional model. I already mentioned given the level of stealth technology the world had before second impact. Hitting a deactivated Eva with a precision strike would be deadly. Once they're AT-field is down they're just like a giant human being. Anyone trying to engage an Eva head-on with conventional forces is stupid. I'm saying human ingenuity would be able to find a way to disable or destroy an Eva with ease. Finally a nuclear weapon is the most dangerous weapon on the planet. Able to wipe out ten's of thousands of people in a second or destroy wide swaths of territory. I'll take that any day. You also act like a a nuclear weapon couldn't destroy an Eva. A N2 mine was able to break Sachiel AT-Field. How do you think we'll do once we start going into high yield range of nuclear weapons.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 04, 2012 7:30 pm Na7e wrote: I still make the case they're awful. You can see them miles away. A berserk unit is even worse. It would attack anything it see's hence why it's called a 'berserker'. Why use a unit that could turn on you. Even then we've only seen two cases of it. Those being a prototype, Test-type, and a provisional model.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 04, 2012 8:27 pm Things are getting muddled here. Sailor Star Dust wrote: In NME with the Third Angel, the Bethany Base Commander was concerned that things weren't going as they should ("Defend the Limbo Area at all costs! We cannot allow it to escape from Acheron! How could a containment system as secure as Cocytus be neutralized?", "Get Unit-05 to do something!"), but IF he's one of the silhouetted Eva Store characters, I wonder if he'd have a chance to show his stuff in 3.0? I do agree Gendo has the most experience (though he normally hands off strategies to Misato), since the Angels attack Tokyo-3 as we've seen. Bethany Base Commander didn't seem to have an Operations Director, then again, Mari was sent out in an emergency situation so...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 04, 2012 8:30 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: Please do not mix up the two continuities, as they are not the same. Doing so will eventually always result in flawed analysis and bad arguments.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 04, 2012 8:52 pm That is reassuring. I have one final thing to say about the souring of the Gendo-SEELE relationship. In NGE, we saw Gendo was travelling a lot during the early part of the show's run. So often, in fact that it almost became a running gag to hear Misato mention every other episode that either herself or Fuyutsuki is left "in charge until Commander Ikari returns from his business trip." Around the time that SEELE started doubting Gendo's motives, he stayed in Tokyo-3. All the time. He seemed to spend nearly all of his time within the confines of the Geofront, given the various shots of him watching Rei soak in yellow fluid. His brief visit to Yui's grave with Shinji kept a helicopter on stand-by to transport him back to base. This behaviour was not so much a security measure to have the Commander standing by for an Angel attack at any moment, but rather a security measure to protect the Commander from abduction by SEELE. In Rebuild, we still see Gendo travelling in 2.0, and 3.0 promises another trip to a desert region. However, his relationship with SEELE hasn't soured yet. They don't like one another, and SEELE's suspicions of Gendo having an alternate agenda have already started. What's interesting is if 3.0 will have Gendo actually replaced, and what will happen after that. My guess (but I emphasize it is no more than that) is that Gendo is questioned, discharged and replaced by the Bethania Base commander, after which Gendo and Fuyutsuki will leave in exile (along with a donkey). After some possibly Angel-related events, the new command staff will unfortunately get killed, which is when Gendo returns. SEELE will be forced to reinstate him either because they need his expertise in commanding Angel-battles, or Gendo manipulates global politics to the extent that the governments in Japan, Europe and North America unanimously back him up. Which will put him back in his iconic place as hierarchical father/leader archetype for the start of FINAL.

[]: Evas; Viable weapons? Somewhat. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Jan 04, 2012 8:53 pm Na7e wrote: The fourth division also placed a naval vessel in a lake on top of a de-activated Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Jan 07, 2012 10:34 am Necro! Necro? Eh, not really, more like CPR. Jornophelanthas wrote: Replacing Gendo would not have been easily done. It's not as if Gendo would show up when summoned;

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jan 07, 2012 10:48 am SaltyJoe wrote: I honest to God don't see what that crucial knowledge was beyond infroamtion that allows him to fuck up their plans. And if all else fails, find a real marionette who can then be stringed along in whatever way Seele sees fit.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Jan 07, 2012 11:30 am Bagheera wrote: I'm starting to see how people feel when I go on my "Gendo is an idiot" rants.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jan 07, 2012 11:40 am SaltyJoe wrote: I mean really, look at EoE: "Kill all the pilots!.... No wait, we'll use the pilot of Unit-01 as the medium for our rituals. Thank God for busty chicks with guns and maternal drives."

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Jan 07, 2012 12:11 pm ^ That would imply that Seele hasn't the faintest clue about the psychological profile of the guy they entrusted with their most precious project. No matter how i look at it, Seele just shakes my faith in competent bad guy-dom.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jan 07, 2012 1:24 pm SaltyJoe wrote: ^ That would imply that Seele hasn't the faintest clue about the psychological profile of the guy they entrusted with their most precious project. No matter how i look at it, Seele just shakes my faith in competent bad guy-dom.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sat Jan 07, 2012 8:55 pm SaltyJoe wrote: "When summoned"? Did i gave the impression that i'm suggesting some formal ways for getting rid of Gendo? Last time i checked, assassinations and/or kidnappings didn't require invitations to be sent out. And it's not like they don't know where to find him. Or how to get close to him, considering that the Geofront was constructed on Seele's orders. I mean, it's not too big a stretch to think that they keep tabs on the place and have the blueprints, is it?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sat Jan 07, 2012 10:24 pm SaltyJoe wrote: Actually, i'm not even sure what Seele wanted to accomplish with Tabby-kun, so i'll abort this line of thought.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Jan 08, 2012 7:07 am symbv wrote: One theory has it that Seele wanted to push the self-hatred, thus death-wish, of Shinji so that when he came out with EVA01 (which Seele now had to get to as well in order to start 3I) he would choose death and thus make 3I a success. I find this idea quite neat as this would explain why Seele made Kaworu like a boy at Shinji's age and sent him to be his classmate and fellow pilot and why Kaworu seemed to understand his mission as to befriend Shinji and get him to view him as a true friend. Of course if Kaworu actually managed to get Lilith or Eva01 it is all the better. So from Seele's point of view, either way they stood to benefit.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Jan 08, 2012 10:28 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: Actually, I think Kaworu's befriending of Shinji was all Kaworu's doing.
